mid.title: Seedance 2.0
mid.score_reason: Seedance 2.0 视频生成模型更新，属于重要模型迭代但非语言模型核心赛道
mid.analysis: Seedance 2.0 是由字节跳动 Seed 研究团队发布的一个视频生成模型。它采用了创新的多阶段训练框架，将视频生成过程分解为语义规划、空间结构和精细细节等阶段，以提升长视频的连贯性与质量。该方法通过分而治之的策略，有效解决了长视频生成中常见的时序不一致和内容突变问题，值得关注。

mid.title: Effective context engineering for AI agentsSep 29, 2025
mid.score_reason: AI agent 的上下文工程方法论，直接改变 agent 系统 prompt 设计与上下文管理的工程实践
mid.analysis: 这篇文章来自Anthropic工程团队，系统阐述了为AI智能体设计有效上下文（context）的工程方法。核心变化在于，它将上下文从静态提示词提升为一种需要精心构建的“工程产物”，强调通过结构化信息、明确指令和动态管理来提升智能体表现的稳定性。这值得关注，因为它直面了当前智能体应用中的核心痛点，即如何让模型在复杂、多步骤任务中保持专注并减少错误，为开发者提供了可操作的实践指南。

engineering.title: Tuning the harness, not the model: a Nemotron 3 Ultra playbook
engineering.summary: We tuned an Nemotron 3 Ultra's harness to match Opus 4.8's best agent run at ~8x lower cost, changing only the scaffolding around it.
engineering.score_reason: 通过调优脚手架而非模型，以 1/8 成本匹配头部模型效果，直接改变 agent 工程实践思路

engineering.title: How to Use RLMs in Deep Agents
engineering.summary: Recursive language models (RLMs) fix context rot by having agents write code that dispatches subagents over context chunks instead of pumping everything in one context window. Deep Agents now implements this through dynamic subagents and a lightweight code interpreter, letting agents programmatically fan out work like grep, map, and reduce over large inputs. We benchmark the approach on OOLONG, a long-context reasoning task, and show it holds up where turn-by-turn agents start to break down.
engineering.score_reason: RLM 解决 context rot 问题，通过子 agent 分片处理大输入，有基准测试，方法新颖可复现

engineering.title: How Deep Agents Run Untrusted Code Without a Sandbox
engineering.summary: A smaller, in-process alternative to full sandboxes: WASM + QuickJS for isolation, least-privilege capabilities, and snapshot-based durable pauses.
engineering.score_reason: WASM+QuickJS 实现进程内沙箱运行不可信代码，解决 agent 安全核心问题，有具体技术方案

engineering.title: Introducing Dynamic Subagents in Deep Agents
engineering.summary: Dynamic subagents let AI agents orchestrate work at scale using code instead of tool calls. Learn how programmatic orchestration in Deep Agents guarantees coverage, handles fan-out, and unlocks reliable multi-step, complex agent pipelines with common orchestration patterns and live traces.
engineering.score_reason: 动态子Agent用代码编排替代工具调用，解决大规模Agent编排的工程痛点，方法新颖可复现

engineering.title: Prompt Caching with Deep Agents
engineering.summary: Learn how Deep Agents uses prompt caching to cut LLM token costs by up to 80% across every major model provider - no extra config required.

engineering.title: The Art of Loop Engineering
engineering.summary: Agents automate real-world work, but reliable performance requires more than a good model, it requires a carefully designed harness built for specific tasks. This post explores the core agent loop, how stacking and extending loops builds more effective agents, and how to instrument each level with LangChain primitives.
engineering.score_reason: 系统讲解 agent loop 工程方法论，包含堆叠扩展和 LangChain 原语实现，对 agent 工程实践有直接指导意义

engineering.title: Agents need their own computer. Here's how to give them one safely.
engineering.summary: For most of computing history, a developer environment meant a physical machine, then a VM, then a container, with each one shared across the work happening on it, each one requiring deliberate setup and teardown. The idea of giving every agent its own isolated computer, booting in under a second and cleaning up when done wasn't practical at scale. Sandboxes give you a safer way to run upgraded workflows, providing you an isolated environment to run tasks that require iteration, verification, and access to the tools a person would normally use, all without requiring human supervision. Every human developer gets a laptop. Every agent can get a computer.
engineering.score_reason: agent 隔离沙箱方案，解决 agent 安全执行的生产实际问题

engineering.title: OpenWiki Brains: Proactive Memory for AI Agents
engineering.summary: OpenWiki Brains turns sources like Gmail, Notion, Git, X, Hacker News, and web search into a local wiki that agents can use as fresh, proactive memory.

engineering.title: Give your agent its own computer
engineering.summary: Running code execution in an AI agent is harder than it looks. Your agent needs a real computer (filesystem, shell, package manager, persistent state) but handing it access to your infrastructure is dangerous.Think about it this way: you use one laptop. You are n of one. But agents are going to run millions of tasks, and each one needs its own computer to work from. That's the infrastructure shift happening right now. Satya Nadella put it plainly: "Every agent needs a computer." The question is what that computer looks like, and how you give it to them safely.LangSmith Sandboxes are our answer to that. Here's why it matters, and why doing it yourself is harder than it sounds.
engineering.score_reason: 讨论 agent 代码执行的安全基础设施问题，提出每个 agent 独立计算环境的思路，解决生产环境真实痛点
